items.title: Open Source AI | Meta
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source AI release is the Llama 4 herd, announced in early 2026. Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick are the first open-weight natively multimodal models from Meta, built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture and pre-trained on over 30 trillion tokens across 200 languages. Llama 4 Behemoth, the largest model in the herd, features 288B active parameters, 16 experts, and nearly two trillion total parameters. The models are available for download on llama.c

items.title: 12+ AI Models in March 2026: The Week That Changed AI
items.author: www.buildfastwithai.com
items.snippet: March 2026 has been an exceptionally active month for open-source AI model releases. Meta's Llama 4 family — including Scout and Maverick variants — launched with native multimodal capabilities (text, images, short video) built on a Mixture-of-Experts architecture. Alibaba released Qwen 3.5 Small with four dense models (0.8B–9B parameters), all natively multimodal and Apache 2.0 licensed; the 9B variant outperforms GPT-OSS-120B on graduate-level reasoning benchmarks. NVIDIA quietly dropped Nemot

items.title: From MCP to multi-agents: The top 10 new open source AI projects on GitHub right now — GitHub Blog
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: The week of March 28, 2026 sees the GitHub AI agent ecosystem dominated by a handful of breakout projects. OpenClaw has emerged as the single fastest-growing open-source project in GitHub history, surpassing 335,000 stars — crossing React's all-time record roughly 60 days after its public relaunch. OpenClaw is an autonomous, locally-running AI agent designed for developer workflow automation, personal productivity, browser control, and proactive scheduling. A long-horizon SuperAgent harness proj

items.title: Cursor 2.3 is here! - Cursor Community Forum
items.author: forum.cursor.com
items.snippet: Cursor has been active with major releases in March 2026. On March 11, they launched 30+ new marketplace plugins from partners including Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Glean, Hugging Face, and PlanetScale, alongside an Automations feature enabling always-on agents triggered by Slack, Linear, GitHub, PagerDuty, and webhooks. On March 19, Cursor introduced Composer 2 with frontier-level coding performance, and on March 25, they launched self-hosted cloud agents that keep code and tool execution entir

items.title: Release v0.35.0 · crewAIInc/crewAI
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: In 2026, the AI agent framework landscape has matured significantly, with LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen/AG2 emerging as the dominant production frameworks. LangGraph reached v1.0 in late 2025 and is now the de facto runtime for all LangChain agents, favored for enterprise deployments requiring compliance, auditability, and human-in-the-loop oversight. CrewAI (now at v0.35+) has added A2A protocol support for agent interoperability and introduced the ability to bring in third-party agents from L

items.title: Architectural Choices in China's Open-Source AI Ecosystem: Building Beyond DeepSeek
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet:
In early 2026, Hugging Face has seen a wave of significant open-source model releases across multiple domains and regions. Meta launched Llama 4 with two variants — Maverick (17B active / ~400B total with 128 experts) and Scout (17B active / ~109B total with 16 experts) — both using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with native multimodality for text and image inputs. This marks a major evolution from Llama 3 and has generated significant community attention on the Hub.

Beyond Meta, the global

items.title: GitHub - salttechno/LLM-Model-Comparison-2026: Compare 16 LLMs for enterprise use
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: As of late March 2026, the open-source LLM landscape is highly active with several major releases and benchmark comparisons. A comprehensive comparison project on GitHub tracks 16 LLMs for enterprise use in 2026, including GPT-4.1, Claude 4.5, Gemini 2.5, Llama 4, DeepSeek, and Mistral, with benchmark scores covering MMLU, HumanEval, MATH, and MT-Bench sourced from official provider documentation and verified third-party evaluations. Notably, Llama 4 and Qwen 3 are highlighted among the top open

items.title: Artificial Intelligence Regulations: State and Federal AI Laws 2026 — Drata
items.author: drata.com
items.snippet: AI regulation is accelerating on both sides of the Atlantic in 2026. The EU AI Act, which entered into force in August 2024, is reaching key milestones: prohibited AI practices became enforceable in February 2025, GPAI model governance rules applied from August 2025, and the full transparency and compliance framework becomes applicable on August 2, 2026. Member states are now standing up national enforcement bodies ahead of full implementation. On the US federal side, President Trump signed an E

items.title: [2603.12710] AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week on arXiv (late March 2026), research into LLM-based AI agents continues at a rapid pace across three major themes. First, planning and web agents: a new paper titled 'AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents' (2603.12710, March 13) formalizes web tasks as sequential decision problems and maps agent architectures to classical search paradigms — BFS, DFS, and Best-First Tree Search — offering a principled taxonomy for understanding how modern agents plan.
